channel.title: Blogs on My New Hugo Project
channel.description: Recent content in Blogs on My New Hugo Project

item.title: How to Track Down Risky Security Group Rules Across All of Your AWS Accounts using Lambda
item.description:
In AWS, we might accidentally leave Security Group rules open to:
0.0.0.0/0.

That means the resource could be reachable from anywhere on the internet. The challenge is not only finding those risky rules, but also understanding the full context in one place:

  • which account owns the Security Group
  • which Security Group ID it is
  • which EC2 instance or resource is attached to it
  • and which Region it is running in

In this project, I built a centralized architecture that can automatically detect these cases across multiple AWS accounts and export a report with all of that information in one setup.
